• 2023 Second-Quarter reported sales growth of 6.

3% to $25.5 Billion with operational growth of 7.5%* and adjusted operational growth of 6.2%*. Operational growth excluding COVID-19 Vaccine of 8.9%*

• Earnings per share (EPS) of $1.96 increasing 8.

• Company  is  increasing  2023  full-year  guidance  midpoints  for  adjusted  operational  sales  excluding COVID-19 Vaccine and adjusted operational EPS

• Johnson & Johnson intends to "split off" Kenvue shares through an exchange offer as the form of its next step in the separation,

 “Our robust performance in the second quarter and first half of 2023 is a testament to the hard work and  commitment  of  our  colleagues  around  the  world, ”  said  Joaquin  Duato,  Chairman  of  the  Board  and  Chief Executive Officer.

19e93be3-5ca8-4930-a35b-4ecd59a06f0e.label: text_chunk
19e93be3-5ca8-4930-a35b-4ecd59a06f0e.text:
Pharmaceutical

Pharmaceutical  worldwide  adjusted  operational  sales  grew  3.9%*.  Excluding  the  COVID-19  Vaccine,  adjusted operational sales grew 6.2%*.

 Growth was driven by DARZALEX (daratumumab), ERLEADA (apalutamide), and CARVYKTI  (ciltacabtagene  autoleucel)  in  Oncology,

71f6dbc3-3b58-475b-9caf-2a9777088bf8.label: text_chunk
71f6dbc3-3b58-475b-9caf-2a9777088bf8.text: STELARA  (ustekinumab)  and  TREMFYA  (guselkumab)  in Immunology,  UPTRAVI  (selexipag)  and  OPSUMIT  (macitentan)  in  Pulmonary  Hypertension,  and  SPRAVATO (esketamine)  in  Neuroscience.

1f0d4047-b881-4aa9-b365-d3fd1b4a97fa.label: text_chunk
1f0d4047-b881-4aa9-b365-d3fd1b4a97fa.text: Growth  was  partially  offset  by  ZYTIGA  (abiraterone  acetate)  and  IMBRUVICA (ibrutinib) in Oncology, COVID-19 Vaccine (Ad26.COV2.S) in Infectious Diseases, and REMICADE (infliximab) in Immunology.

2fb113c9-5d53-4d99-854d-3cac9be2b272.label: text_chunk
2fb113c9-5d53-4d99-854d-3cac9be2b272.text:
MedTech

MedTech  worldwide  adjusted  operational  sales  grew  9.9%*,  driven  primarily  by  electrophysiology  products  in Interventional  Solutions,  trauma  in  Orthopaedics,  wound  closure  products  in  General  Surgery,  biosurgery  in Advanced  Surgery,  and  contact  lenses  in  Vision.  MedTech  worldwide  operational  sales  grew  14.

da03179a-6ee9-41c0-aca5-080f9c626e29.label: text_chunk
da03179a-6ee9-41c0-aca5-080f9c626e29.text:
7%*,  with  the acquisition of Abiomed contributing 4.8%.

Consumer Health

Consumer Health worldwide adjusted operational sales increased 7.7%* largely driven by over-the-counter (OTC) products.  Major contributors to growth in OTC were TYLENOL and MOTRIN analgesics, upper respiratory products, international  smoking  cessation  products,  and  IMODIUM  in  digestive  health  products.

48a4a342-966c-4b7f-ba10-d71c241da67a.label: text_chunk
48a4a342-966c-4b7f-ba10-d71c241da67a.text: Additional  contributors  to growth  were  NEUTROGENA  in  Skin  Health/Beauty  products  and Women’s  Health  products  outside  the  United States.

ABOUT JOHNSON & JOHNSON:

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe good health is the foundation of vibrant lives, thriving communities and forward progress. That’s why for more than 135 years, we have aimed to keep people well at every age and every stage of life.

513097d8-a2d4-4fdb-b639-76bd8b42d15e.label: text_chunk
513097d8-a2d4-4fdb-b639-76bd8b42d15e.text:
Today, as the world’s largest, most diversified healthcare products company, we are committed to using our reach  and  size  for  good.

  We  strive  to  improve  access  and  affordability,  create  healthier  communities,  and  put  a healthy mind, body and environment within reach of everyone, everywhere.  We are blending our heart, science and ingenuity to profoundly change the trajectory of health for humanity.

NOTE TO INVESTORS CONCERNING FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This press release contains “forward-looking statements” as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 regarding, among other things: future operating and financial performance, product development, market position and business strateg y, and the anticipated separation of the Company’s Consumer Health business.  The reader  is  cautioned  not  to  rely  on  these  forward-looking  statements.

2ec22372-ab85-4805-bcf2-2853410eaec5.label: text_chunk
2ec22372-ab85-4805-bcf2-2853410eaec5.text:
These  statements  are  based  on  current expectations  of  future  events.

    If  underlying  assumptions  prove  inaccurate  or  known  or  unknown  risks  or uncertainties materialize, actual results could vary materially from the expectations and projections of Johnson & Johnson.  Risks and uncertainties include, but are not limited to: economic factors, such as interest rate and currency exchange rate fluctuations; competition, including technological advances, new products and patents attained by

30287aa9-4de3-4153-b8d6-37f7af5714c1.label: text_chunk
30287aa9-4de3-4153-b8d6-37f7af5714c1.text: competitors; challenges inherent in new product research and development, including uncertainty of clinical success and obtaining regulatory approvals; uncertainty of commercial success for new and existing products; challenges to patents; the impact of patent expirations; the ability of the company to successfully execute strategic plans, including restructuring  plans;  the  impact  of  business  combinations  and  divestitures;  manufacturing  difficulties  or  delays, internally or within the supply chain;

b866d2c6-83e0-4db2-8dac-8d20772a1155.label: text_chunk
b866d2c6-83e0-4db2-8dac-8d20772a1155.text: product efficacy or safety concerns resulting in product recalls or regulatory action; significant adverse litigation or government action, including related to product liability claims; changes to applicable laws and regulations, including tax laws and global health care reforms; trends toward health care cost containment;  changes  in  behavior  and  spending  patterns  of  purchasers  of  health  care  products  and  services; financial instability of international economies and legal systems and sovereign risk;

c2f46a5f-3276-42e9-a000-7f8956aa7bc9.label: text_chunk
c2f46a5f-3276-42e9-a000-7f8956aa7bc9.text: increased scrutiny of the health car e industry by government agencies; the Company’s ability to satisfy the necessary conditions to consummate the separation  of  the  Company’s  Consumer  Health  business  on  a  timely  basis  or  at  all;  the  Company’s  ability  to successfully  separate  the  Company’s  Consumer  Health  business  and  realize  the  anticipated  benefits  from  the separation;

04b80fdc-667c-4a35-9873-36a4f18a7978.label: text_chunk
04b80fdc-667c-4a35-9873-36a4f18a7978.text: and the New Consumer Health Company’s ability to succeed as a standalone publicly traded company.

COVID-19 Vaccine related costs include remaining commitments and obligations, including external manufacturing network exit costs and required clinical trial expenses, associated with the Company's completion of its COVID-19 vaccine contractual commitments.In the first and second quarter of 2023, the company completed a prioritization of its research and development (R&D) investment within the Pharmaceutical segment to focus on the most promising medicines with the greatest benefit to patients.

95f047f2-87b9-4432-83c8-ad188335302d.label: text_chunk
95f047f2-87b9-4432-83c8-ad188335302d.text:
This resulted in the exit of certain programs within therapeutic areas.

 The R&D program exits are primarily in infectious diseases and vaccines including the discontinuation of its respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) adult vaccine program, hepatitis and HIV development.  The restructuring expenses of $145 million in the quarter ($275 million Q2 YTD) include the termination of partnered and non-partnered program costs and asset impairments.

fbc16ca2-31e6-4026-ad59-20f74b1546bd.label: text_chunk
fbc16ca2-31e6-4026-ad59-20f74b1546bd.text: The tax impact related to special item adjustments reflects the current and deferred income taxes associated with the above pre-tax special items in arriving at adjusted earnings.European Medical Device Regulation (MDR) costs represent one-time compliance costs for the Company’s previously registered products. MDR is a replacement of the existing European Medical Devices Directive regulatory framework, and manufacturers of currently marketed medical devices were required to comply with EU MDR beginning in May 2021.

7f684135-8d09-4194-b015-123a8dd73cc5.label: text_chunk
7f684135-8d09-4194-b015-123a8dd73cc5.text: The Company considers the adoption of EU MDR to be a significant one-time regulatory change and is not indicative of on-going operations.

The summary of relationships highlights the regulatory roles of various entities in overseeing financial and pharmaceutical practices:

1. **United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)**: The SEC regulates the filing of **Form 10-K**, which is a mandatory report for public companies to disclose their financial information. Additionally, it oversees the audits of public companies through the **Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (PCAOB)**, ensuring compliance with federal securities laws.

2. **U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)**: The FDA regulates the **Abbreviated New Drug Application (ANDA)** process, ensuring that generic drugs meet safety and efficacy standards. It also oversees the medication **TRACLEER**, which requires a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) for its distribution.

3. **ASU 2022-04**: This regulation pertains to **Supplier Finance Programs**, mandating disclosure requirements to ensure that buyers provide relevant information about these financial arrangements.

4. **U.S. Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP)**: U.S. GAAP governs the reporting and valuation of **Derivative Instruments** on financial statements, providing the necessary accounting standards.

5. **DRL**: This entity is involved in the regulatory process for drug submissions, specifically for generic versions of the medication **ZYTIGA**.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the critical regulatory frameworks that ensure transparency, safety, and compliance in both financial reporting and pharmaceutical practices.

community_summary.372:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Corporate Structure and Separation**:
   - The Company is planning to separate its Consumer Health business, which will be named **Kenvue**, as part of a corporate restructuring initiative aimed at improving market position and achieving cost savings. This separation is expected to be completed in 2023, pending approval from the Board of Directors and a ruling from the IRS regarding tax implications.

2. **Financial Operations**:
   - The Company has a net debt of **$16.1 billion** and engages in share repurchase programs to manage its equity. It also monitors capital markets for favorable conditions to raise capital and invests excess cash in major banks. The Company is subject to various tax obligations, including compliance with the **Sarbanes-Oxley Act** and the **Exchange Act**, and it processes rebate claims related to its pharmaceutical products.

3. **Legal and Regulatory Challenges**:
   - The Company faces numerous legal challenges, including product liability lawsuits and scrutiny from government agencies regarding its marketing practices. It is involved in ongoing litigation, including cases related to **talc** and **INVOKANA**, and has settled various claims, including a significant settlement with the state of West Virginia. The Company is also subject to investigations by the **Department of Justice** and has received subpoenas related to compliance inquiries.

4. **Product Development and Sales**:
   - The Company develops and maintains a range of consumer products and pharmaceutical offerings, including vaccines and treatments for conditions like schizophrenia. It generated approximately **25% of its sales** in 2022 from new products introduced in the last five years. The Company also provides products to the **Managed Care** program and offers discounts under the **340B program**.

5. **Employee Relations and Corporate Culture**:
   - The Company values its employees and promotes a culture of **Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI)**. It provides guidance through its Credo and Code of Business Conduct and has implemented benefits changes for U.S. non-union employees. The Company also grants Performance Share Units tied to stock performance.

6. **International Operations and Compliance**:
   - The Company operates internationally, including in **Spain**, and is subject to various tax regulations and compliance requirements in different jurisdictions. It has received rulings from the **Swiss Federal Tax Authorities** and is impacted by changes in tax laws, such as the **Tax Cuts and Jobs Act of 2017**.

7. **Risk Management and Cybersecurity**:
   - The Company is aware of risks related to cybersecurity, which could adversely impact its operations and reputation. It handles personal data in compliance with privacy laws and engages with third parties for various operational needs.

8. **Financial Reporting and Auditing**:
   - The Company follows **U.S. GAAP** for financial reporting and has undergone audits by **PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP** to assess the effectiveness of its internal controls. It is responsible for maintaining disclosure controls and procedures to ensure compliance with SEC regulations.

This summary encapsulates the key relationships and operational dynamics of the Company, highlighting its strategic initiatives, financial obligations, legal challenges, and commitment to employee welfare and compliance.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight various financial components and their significance within a company's financial structure. Here’s a summary of the key entities and their relationships:

1. **Accounts Payable** is a component of **Current and Non-Current Liabilities**. It represents a type of liability that contributes to the overall financial obligations of a company.

2. **Inventories** are included in **Total Current Assets**. They are assets that can be converted into cash within a year, thus playing a crucial role in liquidity.

3. **Accrued Liabilities** form part of **Total Current Liabilities**. These represent short-term obligations that the company must settle, indicating immediate financial responsibilities.

4. **Basic Earnings Per Share** is derived from **Net Earnings Per Share**. This metric is essential for assessing the profitability available to common shareholders.

5. **Liabilities** are integral to the **Total** financial metrics, reflecting the overall financial obligations of the entity.

6. **Provision for Taxes on Income** is a component of **Taxes**. It signifies a specific financial obligation that contributes to the total tax liabilities incurred by a company.

7. **Employee Related Obligations** include **Stock-Based Compensation**. This highlights the various forms of compensation that employees may receive, including equity-based incentives.

Overall, these relationships illustrate how different financial elements interconnect, contributing to a comprehensive understanding of a company's financial health and obligations.

The relationships outlined involve key financial concepts related to pension plans, specifically focusing on the Projected Benefit Obligation (PBO), Plan Assets, and their funding status.

1. **Projected Benefit Obligation (PBO) and Plan Assets**: The PBO represents the total amount a pension plan is expected to pay out in the future. It is funded by Plan Assets, which are the resources set aside to meet these future pension payment obligations. This relationship emphasizes the importance of having sufficient assets to cover the projected liabilities.

2. **Funding Relationship**: This reiterates that Plan Assets are essential for funding the PBO, highlighting the critical role of these assets in ensuring that future pension obligations can be met.

3. **Financial Assessment and Over (Under) Funded Status**: The financial health of a pension plan is assessed by comparing the PBO to the actual Plan Assets available. This comparison determines the Over (Under) Funded Status of the plan, indicating whether it is adequately funded to meet its obligations. An overfunded status suggests a surplus of assets, while an underfunded status indicates a shortfall.

In summary, the relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of the Projected Benefit Obligation, Plan Assets, and their funding status, underscoring the necessity for adequate funding to ensure the sustainability of pension plans.

The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight key interactions between various financial concepts, particularly focusing on equity investments, impairments, and classifications of assets and liabilities.

1. **Equity Investments and Impairments**: Equity investments are directly linked to impairments, as changes in their fair value can lead to recorded impairments in financial statements. This indicates that a decline in the value of equity investments results in financial losses that must be acknowledged.

2. **Classification of Financial Instruments**: Equity investments are classified as Level 1 assets, which signifies high liquidity and marketability. In contrast, contingent consideration is classified as Level 3, indicating lower liquidity and marketability. This distinction emphasizes the varying degrees of risk and accessibility associated with these financial instruments.

3. **Assets and Liabilities Classification**: Both assets and liabilities are categorized into different levels based on their characteristics, which also reflects their liquidity and marketability. This classification system is crucial for understanding the financial health and risk profile of an entity.

Overall, the relationships underscore the importance of fair value assessments in equity investments, the implications of impairments on financial reporting, and the significance of classification in evaluating the liquidity and marketability of financial assets and liabilities.

community_summary.724:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Market Exclusivity and Sales**: Market exclusivity enhances sales by preventing competition, allowing exclusive sellers to achieve higher revenues.

2. **Regulatory Action and Products**: Regulatory actions can restrict the marketing and selling capabilities of products, impacting a company's operational effectiveness.

3. **Monetary Policy and Inflation**: Monetary policy influences inflation rates through interest rate adjustments, which can either stimulate or cool economic activity.

4. **Credit Ratings and Liquidity**: Changes in credit ratings affect a company's liquidity by influencing investor confidence and access to capital, essential for maintaining liquid assets.

5. **Generic or Biosimilar Versions and Market Share**: The introduction of generic or biosimilar products can decrease the market share of original products.

6. **Selling, Marketing and Administrative Expenses and Gross Profit**: These expenses reduce overall profitability as they are deducted from gross profit to determine operating income.

7. **Derivatives & Hedges and Comprehensive Income**: Derivatives and hedges can impact comprehensive income through unrealized gains or losses recognized in other comprehensive income.

8. **Credit Losses and Accounts Receivable Allowances**: Higher credit losses directly increase the allowances set aside for uncollectible accounts.

9. **Lease Term and Operating Lease Expense**: The lease term influences the calculation of operating lease expense, recognized over the duration of the lease.

10. **Cross Currency Interest Rate Swaps Contracts and AOCI**: Gains or losses from these contracts are reclassified from Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income (AOCI) into income, indicating a financial relationship.

11. **Treasury Stock Method and Diluted Earnings Per Share**: This method affects the calculation of diluted earnings per share by determining the number of shares repurchased, impacting earnings metrics.

12. **Compensation Cost and Income Tax Benefit**: Share-based compensation costs influence the income tax benefits reported in the income statement.

13. **Infectious Diseases and the U.S.**: Infectious diseases significantly impact the U.S. population, necessitating public health responses and policies for outbreak management.

14. **Cardiovascular and Metabolism**: Cardiovascular diseases are influenced by metabolic processes, highlighting the importance of metabolism in cardiovascular health. 

This summary encapsulates the critical relationships between various entities, emphasizing their significance in economic, health, and financial contexts.

The relationships summarized below highlight various financial comparisons and classifications:

1. **Interest Income and Interest Expense**: These two financial metrics are compared across two fiscal years to assess the financial performance of an entity.

2. **Government Securities vs. Corporate Debt Securities**: Both are types of debt securities, with the key distinction being their issuers; government securities are issued by governments, while corporate debt securities are issued by corporations.

3. **Equity Investments with Readily Determinable Value vs. Equity Investments without Readily Determinable Value**: These categories of equity investments differ based on the ease of determining their fair value, which is crucial for classification and valuation purposes.

4. **Carrying Value vs. Fair Value**: A comparison is made between carrying value and fair value to identify any excess, indicating how much more an asset is valued in the market compared to its recorded value.

5. **Accumulated Benefit Obligation vs. Projected Benefit Obligation**: Both terms pertain to pension obligations; the Accumulated Benefit Obligation reflects total benefits accrued to date, while the Projected Benefit Obligation estimates future benefits.

6. **Numerical Comparison (250 vs. 116)**: The values of 250 and 116 are compared, with 250 being greater than 116.

7. **Fair Value Comparison ($591 million vs. $611 million)**: These figures represent the fair values of restricted share units issued in consecutive fiscal years, facilitating an analysis of the company's compensation strategy over time.
